exemplar.title: What are the differences in cytotoxicity and apparent permeability of two nanoparticle formulations (B1 and B2) with varying ligand concentrations (0%mol, 2%mol, 4%mol, 6%mol, 8%mol, and 10%mol) at five time points (30 min., 45 min., 60 min., 90 min., 120 min.) across the Blood-Brain Barrier, using traditional and inverted in vitro models with bEnd.3 endothelial mouse cells over a 7-day period, for potential Alzheimerâs disease treatment?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Comprehensive, wellâstructured background with accurate definitions and context
Clear tables, figures and glossary make the topic accessible to nonâspecialists
Detailed methodological description with explicit hypotheses guiding the design
weaknesses: Research question is overly broad, covering many variables and time points
Key methodological details omitted (e.g., TEER measurement)
Citation of permeability formula source is missing
Physicochemical characterization of nanoparticles (size, charge) is incomplete
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the background and explanation of topic and methods are exemplary (topâband quality), the diffuse research question and a few methodological gaps prevent a full 6/6. A score of 5 reflects the very strong execution overall, with minor lapses in focus and methodological completeness.

strengths: Terminology and specialized concepts (e.g., TEER, Papp, zeta potential) are used accurately and consistently
Glossary and integrated citations demonstrate coherent and effective use of sources
Core literature on BBB physiology and assay protocols is applied appropriately to justify experimental choices
weaknesses: Some background data rely on outdated sources (e.g., 2017 prevalence statistic)
Critical comparison with the most recent studies is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear, effective use of terminology and generally relevant application of source material. Occasional reliance on dated references and limited critical comparison cap the performance at 5/6 rather than full marks.

strengths: Data are processed methodically with identification of outliers and acknowledgment of experimental error
Discussion links findings back to research aims and proposes realistic methodological improvements
Argumentation is generally coherent, with clear connections between results and conclusions
weaknesses: No statistical significance testing or confidence intervals are presented
Criteria for data exclusion and outlier removal are not quantitatively justified
Negative permeability values are not discussed, leaving interpretive gaps
Some speculative leaps toward clinical application are insufficiently substantiated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis and evaluation are solidly in the goodâtoâveryâgood range, showing consistent relevance to the research question and critical acknowledgement of limitations. However, the absence of inferential statistics and fully rigorous dataâexclusion criteria limits the performance to the upper midâband, reflected in an 8/12.

strengths: Overall report structure is clear and logically ordered with comprehensive table of contents
Consistent formatting (font, spacing, page numbering) facilitates readability
weaknesses: Several figures are pixelated or lack clear legends, hindering interpretation
Long tables disrupt narrative flow and are not moved to appendices
Inconsistent indentation and duplicated captions reduce layout polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear and appropriate structural organization (topâband), but multiple layout and formatting lapses (pixelated images, unwieldy tables, caption issues) limit this to a solid 3/4 rather than full marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report exhibits a well-organised structure: title page, abstract substitutes (implicit), contents table, sequentially numbered sections, appendices and bibliography allow the reader to navigate easily. Headings align with the research process and signpost argument development. Minor omissions (e.g., lack of explicit abstract, some disjointed subsection numbering) prevent a perfect execution, but the structure remains **clear and appropriate throughout**, warranting the top mark in the 3-4 band.

The student applies standard EE formatting conventions: consistent font, 1.5 line spacing, page numbering, section titles and embedded figures/tables. However, several images are noticeably pixelated, some graphs present single-sided error bars, and occasional indentation inconsistencies occur. Figure captions are sometimes duplicated, and very long tables disrupt layout on the page. These lapses mean that layout considerations are only **partially** successful, matching the 1-2 band at its upper point (score 2).

The essay follows a logical progression from background to methods, results, discussion and conclusion, and repeatedly references the three stated goals. However, the expansive research question leads to uneven depthâsome strands (e.g., cytotoxicity trends) are examined in detail, whereas others (e.g., influence of inverted vs. normal models) receive limited synthesis. Speculative leaps to clinical application are not fully substantiated. These issues create *minor inconsistencies* in an otherwise clear argument, fitting the mid-range of the 4-6 band (score 4).

The student provides an extensive, well-structured background section that defines all key terms (e.g., receptor-mediated transcytosis, ligand density) and situates the investigation within current Alzheimerâs and BBB literature. Clear tables, figures and a detailed glossary ensure that a non-specialist reader can follow the scientific context without difficulty. Minor lapses (occasional unfinished sentences, slightly dated prevalence statistic) do not materially impede clarity or comprehensiveness, so the work meets the descriptor for the top band.

Core literature on BBB physiology, nanomedicine approaches and assay protocols is used appropriately to frame and support the investigation. Citations are generally well integrated and linked to experimental choices (e.g., selection of bEnd.3 cells, ligand density rationale). However, some key data (e.g., current AD prevalence, nanoparticle physicochemical characterisation) rely on limited or outdated sources, and critical comparison of findings with recent studies is limited. These issues reduce effectiveness to âmostly relevantâ, consistent with the 3-4 band at the upper level (4).

The data are processed methodically: calibration curves are generated, apparent permeability calculated with correct formulae, and raw data with replicates are provided. The analysis is *clearly relevant* to the question, identifying outliers and acknowledging experimental error (e.g., membrane integrity, pipette accuracy). Nonetheless, absence of inferential statistics (e.g., hypothesis tests, confidence intervals) and limited treatment of variability constrain depth. Overall, the analysis reaches the upper end of the 4-6 band (score 6) rather than demonstrating the consistently sophisticated insight required for 7-9.

The methodology is described in impressive detail: full materials lists, variable tables, safety considerations, and step-by-step SOPs appear in both text and appendix. The choice of *in-vitro* BBB transwell models, MTT assay and dextran/nanoparticle permeability studies is well justified and feasible for a school laboratory with external mentorship. Nevertheless, some rationale gaps remain (e.g., exclusion of astrocytes/pericytes; absence of TEER measurement; limited explanation of ethical approval), preventing the most exemplary (6) award. The strand therefore fits comfortably at the upper end of the 5-6 band but with a slight deduction to 5.

The discussion links results to the research aims, noting that B1-10 shows the highest Papp and that B1-2 may be cytotoxic; methodological limitations (e.g., lack of TEER, variability between transwells) are acknowledged, and realistic improvements proposed. However, evaluation remains largely qualitativeâno statistical significance testing is presented, and the implications for Alzheimerâs therapy are somewhat speculative. These factors position the work solidly in the âgood evaluation with well-supported conclusionsâ descriptor (4-6 band), but short of the highest levels of critical appraisal; a mid-high score of 5 is appropriate.

The research question is explicitly stated and logically derived from the background reading; however, its length and inclusion of multiple variables (two formulations, six ligand densities, five time points, two models, two assays, therapeutic context) create a *diffuse* focus. While the core aimâcomparing cytotoxicity and permeability of B1 vs B2 nanoparticles across BBB modelsâis recognizable, the breadth makes it challenging to address each element in depth. Hence, the question is **clear but only partially focused**, aligning with the 3-4 band.

Throughout the essay the student employs specialised vocabularyâTEER, Papp, ligand mol %, transcytosis, zeta potentialâaccurately and consistently. Definitions are collected in a comprehensive glossary and symbols are used correctly in equations. Occasional typographical slips and a few ambiguous phrases (e.g., âpigment trespasses the membraneâ) are present but do not compromise overall precision. The work satisfies the expectations of clear, **accurate and consistent** terminology, meriting placement in the 5-6 band; a score of 5 recognises very strong but not flawless execution.

annotations.comment: The justification for choosing a single-cell type BBB model is well explained, demonstrating sound rationale for experimental design within resource constraints.
Linking low permeability results to the necessity for nanoparticle redesign shows good critical thinking in assessing therapeutic feasibility.
Subtracting non-specific interactions by using the 0% ligand control is logical, but the calculation method should be described in detail to ensure clarity.
Outlining the source of the permeability formula would strengthen methodological transparency; cite the original publication for $$Pe$$ calculation.
The table of variables for the MTT assay needs clearer distinction between controlled and monitored variables; ensure each category aligns with experimental practice.
The explicit statement of hypothesis and experimental goals demonstrates effective focus and guides subsequent methodological choices.
The process for discarding high-calibration values is not quantitatively justified; clarify the rationale behind data exclusion to avoid bias.
The inclusion of a precise word count at the beginning demonstrates compliance with formatting requirements and confirms that the investigation remains within the 4000-word limit.
The correlation coefficient threshold for calibration curve inclusion is not documented in the methodology; specify criteria for omitting data points.
Criteria for discarding outlier wells in the MTT assay are not defined; include explicit statistical thresholds to improve reproducibility.
A clear, descriptive title is presented at the start, effectively conveying the scope and context of the investigation.
The research question is excessively broad, encompassing numerous variables and time points; consider narrowing the focus to two or three key variables to ensure depth of analysis.
The extensive glossary demonstrates precise and consistent use of specialized terminology, aiding reader comprehension.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly formatted, facilitating easy navigation through sections and subsections.
In the recommendation to adopt more complex BBB models, specify which additional cell types or structural components would most effectively enhance physiological relevance.
The prevalence statistic from van Dyck (2017) is somewhat dated; updating this figure with a more recent WHO report would strengthen the relevance of background data.
Figure 1 is informative but the image appears pixelated. Use higher-resolution graphics to maintain clarity in detailed diagrams.
TEER (transendothelial electrical resistance) measurements are omitted; incorporating TEER would validate monolayer integrity alongside permeability assays.
The line graph contains multiple datasets with similar markers but lacks a clear legend or color key, which hinders reader interpretation.
The discussion acknowledges multiple sources of experimental error and proposes increased replicates and model complexity, demonstrating critical evaluation of limitations.
Figure 3 caption should be placed below the figure and formatted consistently; currently the caption is separated from the image, reducing readability.
Negative apparent permeability values for B1-2 are not addressed; provide discussion on the biological or methodological causes of negative $$P_{app}$$.
The conclusionâs assertion that no trends exist should be supported by statistical analysis; consider including trendlines or significance testing to substantiate this claim.
The examination date is clearly indicated, ensuring completeness of the administrative information without detracting from the academic content.
The 'Monitored variables' table spans the entire page and interrupts narrative flow; consider moving large tables to an appendix or summarizing key points.
Nanoparticle formulations lack key physicochemical details (e.g., size distribution, surface charge) that are essential for interpreting permeability and cytotoxicity results.
The materials table has an empty first column and spans multiple pages, disrupting the layout; consider simplifying or splitting it into categorized lists.
